mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, consumer spending capacity collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, forcing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0% during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5% coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in greater than 70% of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate wage income and consumer demand expectations, mechanically generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions yield negative 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of cases across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 220.0, inflation expectations anchor higher, forcing markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 220.0 are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 65% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient with p-value above 0.10 in predicting 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal immediate manufacturing contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO month-over-month changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of observations during contraction phases.
